title: Prune stale immutable ConfigMaps after deployment
description:
# GitHub Issue #134: Prune stale immutable ConfigMaps after Deployment rollout

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: reconcileConfig creates new immutable ConfigMaps with content-hash suffixes on every config change but never deletes old ones. Add a pruning step that lists ConfigMaps matching the naming pattern, identifies those no longer referenced by any active Pod or ReplicaSet, and deletes them. Retain the last N (e.g., 3) for rollback safety.

**Motivation**: Over the operator's lifetime, orphaned ConfigMaps accumulate indefinitely. This wastes etcd storage and clutters namespace listings, making debugging harder.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go`
- `internal/common/config/`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] After a successful Deployment rollout, unreferenced ConfigMaps older than the current one are deleted
- [ ] The last 3 ConfigMaps are retained for rollback
- [ ] Pruning is idempotent and safe against concurrent reconcile loops
- [ ] Unit tests validate pruning logic

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/134

stories.title: Cluster admin sees clean namespace after config changes
stories.want: orphaned immutable ConfigMaps to be automatically cleaned up after configuration changes roll out
stories.so_that: etcd storage is not wasted and namespace listings remain uncluttered for debugging
criteria: After a successful Deployment rollout with a new ConfigMap, old ConfigMaps beyond the retain count are deleted
Only ConfigMaps matching the baseName-{hash} pattern owned by the Keystone CR are deleted
ConfigMaps created by other controllers or users in the same namespace are never affected
Pruned ConfigMap names are logged at info level for auditability

stories.title: Operator retains recent ConfigMaps for rollback safety
stories.want: the 3 most recent historical ConfigMaps to be retained after pruning
stories.so_that: I can safely roll back to a previous configuration if the new one causes issues
criteria: After pruning, exactly 3 historical ConfigMaps plus the current active one remain (4 total)
The retained ConfigMaps are the 3 most recently created (by CreationTimestamp)
When fewer than 3 historical ConfigMaps exist, no ConfigMaps are deleted
The current active ConfigMap (referenced by Deployment) is never a deletion candidate

stories.title: Pruning does not delete ConfigMaps during active rollout
stories.want: ConfigMap pruning to occur only after the Deployment rollout completes successfully
stories.so_that: ConfigMaps still referenced by pods in old ReplicaSets are never deleted during rollout
criteria: Pruning is not called when reconcileDeployment returns WaitingForDeployment (not ready)
Pruning is not called during upgrade phase transitions (RollingUpdate → Contracting)
Pruning runs only after EnsureDeployment returns ready=true and the reconcile loop proceeds past the deployment check
The pruning call is placed after reconcileDeployment in the main reconcile loop

stories.title: Pruning is safe under concurrent reconciliation
stories.want: ConfigMap pruning to be idempotent and safe against concurrent reconcile loops
stories.so_that: no errors or data loss occur when multiple reconcile goroutines run simultaneously
criteria: Deleting an already-deleted ConfigMap (NotFound) does not return an error
Running prune twice with the same state produces the same result
No race conditions between listing and deleting ConfigMaps cause unexpected failures
Pruning uses client.IgnoreNotFound for delete operations

stories.title: Pruning handles edge cases without errors
stories.want: ConfigMap pruning to gracefully handle all edge cases
stories.so_that: the reconcile loop never fails due to unexpected pruning states
criteria: Empty ConfigMap list (no historical ConfigMaps) is a no-op returning nil
Fewer historical ConfigMaps than retain count is a no-op returning nil
ConfigMaps with no owner reference are skipped (not deleted)
ConfigMaps owned by a different Keystone CR in the same namespace are skipped

requirements.description: After a successful Deployment rollout, the operator SHALL delete immutable ConfigMaps that match the {baseName}-{hash} pattern, are owned by the same Keystone CR, and are not the currently active ConfigMap
requirements.rationale: Prevents unbounded accumulation of orphaned ConfigMaps that waste etcd storage and clutter namespace listings

scenarios.name: Stale ConfigMaps deleted after rollout
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment confirms the Deployment is ready and there are 6 ConfigMaps matching the baseName pattern (1 current + 5 historical)
scenarios.then: the 2 oldest historical ConfigMaps are deleted (retaining current + 3 historical = 4 total)
and_then: no error is returned from pruning

scenarios.name: No pruning when deployment is not ready
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment returns WaitingForDeployment (not ready)
scenarios.then: the reconcile loop returns early with RequeueAfter and pruning is never called

scenarios.name: Pruning error propagated to reconcile loop
scenarios.when: the Kubernetes API returns an unexpected error during ConfigMap deletion
scenarios.then: the error is wrapped and returned from the reconcile loop

requirements.description: The operator SHALL retain the N most recent historical ConfigMaps by CreationTimestamp (where N defaults to 3), in addition to the currently active ConfigMap
requirements.rationale: Provides rollback safety — the operator can revert to any of the N previous configurations without manual intervention

scenarios.name: Newest historical ConfigMaps are retained
scenarios.when: pruning runs with 5 historical ConfigMaps and retain=3
scenarios.then: the 3 newest historical ConfigMaps by CreationTimestamp are kept and the 2 oldest are deleted

scenarios.name: Ordering respects CreationTimestamp not name
scenarios.when: historical ConfigMaps have names that sort alphabetically different from their creation order
scenarios.then: retention is based on CreationTimestamp, not alphabetical name order

scenarios.name: Retain count of zero keeps only current
scenarios.when: pruning runs with retain=0 and 3 historical ConfigMaps
scenarios.then: all 3 historical ConfigMaps are deleted, only the current one remains

requirements.description: ConfigMap pruning SHALL be idempotent — calling prune multiple times with the same cluster state SHALL produce the same result without errors
requirements.rationale: The reconcile loop may run multiple times for the same state; idempotency prevents cascading failures from repeated pruning attempts

scenarios.name: Second prune call is a no-op
scenarios.when: prune runs successfully, then runs again with the same state
scenarios.then: the second call returns nil and no additional ConfigMaps are deleted

scenarios.name: Concurrent deletion handled gracefully
scenarios.when: a ConfigMap is deleted between the List and Delete calls (e.g., by a concurrent reconcile loop)
scenarios.then: the NotFound error from Delete is silently ignored via client.IgnoreNotFound

scenarios.name: Already-pruned state is a no-op
scenarios.when: prune runs when all ConfigMaps are within the retain count
scenarios.then: no delete calls are made and nil is returned

requirements.description: Pruning SHALL only delete ConfigMaps whose controller owner reference UID matches the owning Keystone CR's UID
requirements.rationale: Prevents accidental deletion of ConfigMaps owned by other controllers or manually created by users in the same namespace

scenarios.name: ConfigMaps owned by different CR are skipped
scenarios.when: the namespace contains ConfigMaps matching the baseName pattern but owned by a different Keystone CR
scenarios.then: those ConfigMaps are not included in the pruning candidates

scenarios.name: ConfigMaps without owner reference are skipped
scenarios.when: a ConfigMap matches the baseName pattern but has no controller owner reference
scenarios.then: it is excluded from pruning candidates

scenarios.name: ConfigMaps owned by correct CR are pruned
scenarios.when: a stale ConfigMap matches the baseName pattern and is owned by the target CR
scenarios.then: it is included in the pruning candidates and may be deleted if beyond the retain count

requirements.description: The currently active ConfigMap (identified by currentName parameter) SHALL never be deleted, regardless of the retain count or CreationTimestamp
requirements.rationale: Deleting the active ConfigMap would break the running Deployment and all Pods referencing it

scenarios.name: Current ConfigMap excluded from candidates
scenarios.when: prune is called with currentName matching an existing ConfigMap
scenarios.then: that ConfigMap is excluded from the candidate list before sorting and retention logic

scenarios.name: Current ConfigMap safe even with retain=0
scenarios.when: prune is called with retain=0
scenarios.then: all historical ConfigMaps are deleted but the current one remains

requirements.description: Pruning SHALL be a no-op (no deletions, no errors) when the number of historical ConfigMaps is less than or equal to the retain count
requirements.rationale: Prevents unnecessary API calls and ensures the function is safe to call unconditionally in the reconcile loop

scenarios.name: Fewer historical than retain count
scenarios.when: there are 2 historical ConfigMaps and retain=3
scenarios.then: no ConfigMaps are deleted and nil is returned

scenarios.name: Exactly at retain count
scenarios.when: there are 3 historical ConfigMaps and retain=3
scenarios.then: no ConfigMaps are deleted and nil is returned

scenarios.name: No historical ConfigMaps
scenarios.when: only the current ConfigMap exists
scenarios.then: no ConfigMaps are deleted and nil is returned

requirements.description: Pruning SHALL only be invoked in the reconcile loop after reconcileDeployment confirms the Deployment is ready (IsDeploymentReady returns true)
requirements.rationale: During a rollout, old ReplicaSet pods may still reference old ConfigMaps; premature deletion could cause volume mount failures for pods being terminated

scenarios.name: Pruning runs after deployment readiness
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment returns (ctrl.Result{}, nil) indicating readiness
scenarios.then: the reconcile loop proceeds to pruneStaleConfigMaps

scenarios.name: Pruning skipped during upgrade rollout phase
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment returns Requeue during RollingUpdate→Contracting transition
scenarios.then: the reconcile loop exits early and pruning is not reached

requirements.description: ConfigMaps that do not match the baseName prefix pattern SHALL be left untouched by pruning
requirements.rationale: The namespace may contain ConfigMaps created by other controllers, users, or for other purposes that must not be affected

scenarios.name: Different prefix not matched
scenarios.when: a ConfigMap named 'other-config-abc12345' exists and pruning runs for baseName 'test-keystone-config'
scenarios.then: the ConfigMap is not included in pruning candidates

scenarios.name: Overlapping prefix correctly handled
scenarios.when: ConfigMaps 'test-keystone-config-abc' and 'test-keystone-config-extra-def' exist and pruning runs for baseName 'test-keystone-config'
scenarios.then: only 'test-keystone-config-abc' matches (baseName + '-' prefix); 'test-keystone-config-extra-def' does not

scenarios.name: Exact baseName without hash is not matched
scenarios.when: a ConfigMap named exactly 'test-keystone-config' exists (no hash suffix)
scenarios.then: it is not included in pruning candidates because it does not match baseName + '-' prefix pattern with hash suffix

tasks.title: Add PruneImmutableConfigMaps function to config package with unit tests (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Add `PruneImmutableConfigMaps(ctx, c, owner, baseName, namespace, currentName string, retain int) error` to `internal/common/config/config.go`. The function: (1) lists all ConfigMaps in namespace via c.List, (2) filters to those owned by owner (matching UID) AND having name prefix baseName+'-' AND name != currentName, (3) sorts candidates by CreationTimestamp descending, (4) if len(candidates) <= retain returns nil, (5) deletes candidates[retain:] using client.IgnoreNotFound for idempotency. Add the following unit tests to `internal/common/config/config_test.go`: TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_deletesStaleConfigMaps, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_retainsNewestByCreationTimestamp, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_retainZeroDeletesAllHistorical, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_idempotentOnSecondCall, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_ignoresNotFoundOnDelete, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_skipsConfigMapsOwnedByDifferentController, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_skipsConfigMapsWithoutOwnerReference, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_neverDeletesCurrentConfigMap, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_noopWhenFewerThanRetain, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_noopWhenNoHistoricalConfigMaps, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_skipsMismatchedPrefix, TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_handlesOverlappingPrefixCorrectly. Follow the existing test pattern: NewGomegaWithT(t), fake.NewClientBuilder, pre-populate ConfigMaps with explicit CreationTimestamps via WithObjects.

tasks.title: Add integration tests for PruneImmutableConfigMaps using envtest (REQ-001, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Add TestIntegration_PruneImmutableConfigMaps and TestIntegration_PruneImmutableConfigMaps_idempotent to `internal/common/config/integration_test.go`. Follow the existing pattern: envtestutil.SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable(t), envtestutil.SetupEnvTest(t), create owner ConfigMap, create 5 ConfigMaps with CreateImmutableConfigMap using different data, call PruneImmutableConfigMaps with retain=3, verify 4 remain (current + 3 historical). For idempotency test, call PruneImmutableConfigMaps twice and verify same result.

tasks.title: Wire pruning into keystone controller after deployment readiness with unit tests (REQ-001, REQ-007)
tasks.description: In `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go`: (1) add constant `defaultConfigMapRetainCount = 3`, (2) add method `pruneStaleConfigMaps(ctx, keystone, currentConfigMapName) error` that calls `config.PruneImmutableConfigMaps(ctx, r.Client, keystone, fmt.Sprintf("%s-config", keystone.Name), keystone.Namespace, currentConfigMapName, defaultConfigMapRetainCount)`. In `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`: add the pruning call after the reconcileDeployment check (line ~141) as `if err := r.pruneStaleConfigMaps(ctx, &keystone, configMapName); err != nil { return r.updateStatus(ctx, &keystone, ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf("pruning stale ConfigMaps: %w", err)) }`. Add unit tests to `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config_test.go`: TestPruneStaleConfigMaps_deletesOldConfigMaps, TestPruneStaleConfigMaps_retainsRecentConfigMaps, TestPruneStaleConfigMaps_noopWithNoCandidates. Follow existing test helpers: configTestScheme(), configTestKeystone(), newConfigTestReconciler().

tasks.title: Update reference documentation for ConfigMap pruning (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Update `docs/reference/kubernetes-packages.md`: add a PruneImmutableConfigMaps section after the existing CreateImmutableConfigMap section, documenting the function signature, parameters table, behavior description, and usage example (follow the CreateImmutableConfigMap section format). Update `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md`: add a 'ConfigMap Pruning' subsection in the reconcileConfig section documenting that stale ConfigMaps are pruned after deployment rollout with retain count of 3, and update the reconcile flow diagram/table to include the pruning step after reconcileDeployment.

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin sees clean namespace after config changes
test_specifications.expected: With 5 historical ConfigMaps and retain=3, the 2 oldest are deleted and 3 newest remain

test_specifications.story: Operator retains recent ConfigMaps for rollback safety
test_specifications.expected: ConfigMaps are sorted by CreationTimestamp and the N newest are retained, regardless of name ordering

test_specifications.story: Operator retains recent ConfigMaps for rollback safety
test_specifications.expected: With retain=0, all historical ConfigMaps are deleted, only current remains

test_specifications.story: Pruning is safe under concurrent reconciliation
test_specifications.expected: Second call with same state returns nil without additional deletes

test_specifications.story: Pruning is safe under concurrent reconciliation
test_specifications.expected: When a ConfigMap is deleted between List and Delete, the NotFound error is silently ignored
ConfigMaps owned by a different controller UID are not deleted even if they match the baseName prefix
ConfigMaps with no owner reference are excluded from pruning candidates

test_specifications.story: Operator retains recent ConfigMaps for rollback safety
test_specifications.expected: The ConfigMap matching currentName is never in the candidate list, even with retain=0

test_specifications.story: Pruning handles edge cases without errors
test_specifications.expected: With 2 historical ConfigMaps and retain=3, no deletions occur and nil is returned

test_specifications.story: Pruning handles edge cases without errors
test_specifications.expected: When only the current ConfigMap exists, no deletions occur and nil is returned
ConfigMaps with a different baseName prefix are not included in pruning candidates
baseName 'test-config' matches 'test-config-abc' but not 'test-config-extra-def' (strict baseName+'-' prefix)

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin sees clean namespace after config changes
test_specifications.expected: Full pruning lifecycle with real envtest API server: create 5 ConfigMaps, prune, verify 4 remain (current + 3 historical)

test_specifications.story: Pruning is safe under concurrent reconciliation
test_specifications.expected: Calling prune twice against envtest produces same result without errors

test_specifications.story: Cluster admin sees clean namespace after config changes
test_specifications.expected: pruneStaleConfigMaps on the keystone controller deletes ConfigMaps beyond the retain count using the correct baseName pattern

test_specifications.story: Operator retains recent ConfigMaps for rollback safety
test_specifications.expected: pruneStaleConfigMaps retains the 3 most recent historical ConfigMaps and the current one

test_specifications.story: Pruning handles edge cases without errors
test_specifications.expected: pruneStaleConfigMaps returns nil when only the current ConfigMap exists

review_criteria: PruneImmutableConfigMaps correctly deletes ConfigMaps beyond the retain count, sorted by CreationTimestamp (oldest first)
The currently active ConfigMap (currentName) is never included in deletion candidates, even with retain=0
Only ConfigMaps owned by the target CR (matching UID) and matching the baseName+'-' prefix are candidates for pruning
Pruning is called only after reconcileDeployment confirms the Deployment is ready — verify placement in keystone_controller.go
`go vet` and `golangci-lint run` produce no new warnings in modified files
Documentation in kubernetes-packages.md includes PruneImmutableConfigMaps function reference and keystone-reconciler.md includes pruning in the reconcile flow
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

The pruning utility is placed in `internal/common/config/config.go` alongside `CreateImmutableConfigMap` because pruning is the natural complement to creation and follows the same baseName+hash naming convention. The keystone controller calls it via a thin wrapper method `pruneStaleConfigMaps`.

## Data Flow

```
reconcileConfig() → creates {name}-config-{hash} ConfigMap
         ↓
reconcileDeployment() → updates Deployment volume with new ConfigMap name
         ↓ (only when Deployment is ready)
pruneStaleConfigMaps() → lists all {name}-config-* ConfigMaps owned by CR
         ↓                  → sorts by CreationTimestamp desc
         ↓                  → deletes candidates[retain:]
         ↓
reconcileHealthCheck() → continues normal reconcile flow
```

## Key Decisions

1. **Retain count = 3 historical** (constant, not CRD-configurable): Keeps current + 3 historical = 4 total. Sufficient for rollback. CRD configurability deferred to avoid scope creep.

2. **Ownership-based filtering** (not label-based): ConfigMaps created by `CreateImmutableConfigMap` have no labels, only owner references. Filtering by controller owner UID is the correct approach.

3. **Name prefix matching**: Uses `strings.HasPrefix(cm.Name, baseName+"-")` which correctly matches `test-keystone-config-a1b2c3d4` but not `test-keystone-config-extra-def` because the hash is always exactly 8 chars after the baseName.

4. **Placement after reconcileDeployment**: The pruning call is placed after the deployment readiness check in `keystone_controller.go`. This ensures all pods are running the new config before old ConfigMaps are deleted.

5. **Error handling**: Pruning errors are returned to the reconcile loop (standard pattern), causing controller-runtime to requeue with backoff. This is safe because all preceding steps are idempotent.

## Potential Pitfalls

- **Fake client CreationTimestamp**: The fake client does not auto-set CreationTimestamp on Create. Unit tests must pre-populate ConfigMaps with explicit timestamps via WithObjects.
- **Prefix overlap**: `baseName+"-"` prefix matching handles overlapping names correctly (e.g., `test-config-` does not match `test-config-extra-`).
- **Immutable ConfigMaps in running pods**: Deleting an immutable ConfigMap does not affect already-mounted volumes. Kubelet does not sync immutable ConfigMap volumes after initial mount.
